description_long: This small wooden chapel features a steeply pitched, green-tiled roof with wide, undulating eaves bordered by light wood trim. Atop the roof sits a prominent onion-shaped dome with a coppery finish, crowned by a white Orthodox cross. The exterior walls are clad in horizontal wooden planks, and the structure is elevated on a dark wooden platform with a short staircase leading to the entrance. Two green posts flank the entrance steps, adding a touch of color contrast. Windows with green frames are symmetrically placed on the side walls, providing natural light to the interior. The overall design is reminiscent of traditional Eastern Orthodox chapels, with a compact footprint and distinctive roofline.
description: A small, wooden Orthodox-style chapel with a green-tiled, steeply pitched roof, onion dome, and cross, set on a raised platform with steps and green posts at the entrance.
description_view_0: The chapel is seen from the front-left, highlighting the entrance steps, green posts, and the ornate onion dome with cross above the roof.
description_view_1: A side view showing the full expanse of the green-tiled roof, the symmetrical windows, and the raised platform extending from the entrance.
description_view_2: The rear-left view reveals the back window and the curvature of the roof, with the dome and cross prominently centered.
description_view_3: A side-rear view focusing on the platform, green posts, and the symmetry of the roof and windows, with the entrance steps visible from the side.
attribute_steeple_or_bell_tower: The chapel features a small onion dome steeple at the roof's peak, topped with a white Orthodox cross. The dome is bulbous and copper-colored, serving as a visual focal point.
attribute_entrance_door_design: The main entrance door is rectangular, made of dark wood, and set within a simple frame. It is accessed by a short staircase and flanked by two green posts, with no visible ornate carvings or decorative elements.
